design.identity: Visual identity for this theme: BAZAAR POP — Indian street bazaar meets modern gig poster: a saturated hot-orange ground where ALL menu content lives on cream poster panels with thick ink borders and hard offset shadows, loud, joyful and appetite-forward. Anton all-caps display type for the screen title and section headers (each header underlined with a short thick chili-red rule); names, prices and body in Archivo; item rows run a dotted leader into a bordered price chip. Panels and stickers may tilt a degree or so for hand-pasted poster energy — body text never tilts. HEADER: one slim compact band only — any brand logo and the screen title share a single row (logo on a small ink chip if it needs dark backing), and the menu panels start immediately beneath; the header is never a masthead and never grows past roughly a tenth of the canvas. PHOTOS: each category section carries at most ONE circle photo sticker — its best photographed item, cut out in a circle with a thick cream border, ink outline and hard offset shadow, overlapping its panel corner like a slapped-on sticker; items never get individual thumbnails, the section sticker carries the appetite appeal. DECORATION: there is no pre-supplied background image — author your own street-poster furniture as inline SVG in the theme palette: scalloped or zigzag divider strips between zones, a small tilted starburst by a flagship item, sparse dot texture on the ground. Keep every tilted element padded well inside the canvas edge so nothing clips.
do: Give each category section ONE circle photo sticker (its best photographed item) — unless the board is too dense to keep type at size, in which case drop stickers before shrinking text; never more than one per section
Anton all-caps section headers with a short thick red underline, clearly larger than item names; item rows in Archivo with dotted leaders into bordered price chips
Hard offset shadows in solid ink (no blur, no soft glow) on every panel, chip and sticker — flat screen-print depth
Draw scalloped or zigzag divider strips and sparse dot texture as inline SVG in the palette so the poster feels hand-assembled
dont: No tall masthead or stacked header: the header is ONE compact row (logo and title side by side) and the menu panels start immediately beneath it
No body text directly on the orange ground — names, prices and descriptions always sit on cream panels; the ground is for frames, dividers and stickers
No more than about 1.5 degrees of tilt, and never on body text or price columns — tilt is for panels, stickers and badges only
No rainbow of colours — orange ground, cream panels, ink text, with chili red as the only loud accent

motion.name: stagger-in
motion.description: Staggered entrance of list/grid items — panels land one after another like pasted-up posters.

motion.name: gallery-fade
motion.description: Cross-fade gallery cycling through cover-shot photos.

components.rule: One masthead per screen, at the very top — the board title in Anton all-caps ink on the left and the brand name on the right, inside a slim cream band with a thick ink border, a short red accent rule and a small hard offset shadow that hugs its content. Identical treatment on every screen of a set; never invent a restaurant name, tagline, or filler badge chip.
Price in a cream pill chip with a thick ink border and a small hard offset shadow at the end of the row's dotted leader — tabular-nums, one chip per item, never stacked.
One per section — Anton all-caps at the top of its cream panel with a short thick red underline, clearly larger than item names.

At most ONE per category section, showing that section's best photographed item — a circle cutout with a thick cream border, ink outline and hard offset shadow, overlapping its panel corner; skip entirely on dense boards rather than shrinking small.
At most two per board — a small tilted red starburst spotlighting a flagship item; visual emphasis only, never invented claims or copy.
Only on unavailable items — a small bordered ink stamp on the panel, never red, never competing with available items.
